content_of_post_to_comment:
My job is hybrid-remote, meaning I go into the office 3x a week. Another thing about this job is there is a decent amount of down time, I'm not complaining about that by any means but more so I am setting a stage for why I spend so much time browsing the internet. What I have discovered is a vast culture of negativity. Everything is negative, everyone is mad, everyone has such strong opinions, everyone name calls and belittles their fellow humans.

I realized this is a problem when I took a week off, I spent last week hiking through parts of Appalachia. For a lot of this I had no cell service and for the times I did I just didn't care to look because of the beauty that was around me. What I found was kind people, beautiful sights, hard trails, and support from strangers. The contrast to what I find on the internet was staggering, I must have met and talked to 50 strangers and all of them were kind.

I can see the comments now "OP discovers real life" haha maybe true. However, I do spend time off the internet each day, I walk in the park, I lift, I run, and I go to bars and stuff with friends. I think what it took for me to really be able to see through the veil was that entire week off, I felt so good without all the noise and bs that being online brings. It felt like my world shrunk, in a good way, like everything that mattered was right in front of me.

Maybe you guys can relate, I'm just so sick of the negativity.
